persona: Someone narrating workplace drama and seeking validation

turns.content: Hey, I'm so glad I'm talking to you, I've been dying to share all this drama that's been going on at work. So, like, my coworker, Rachel, she's been acting super weird lately, always whispering to our boss and giving me these side-eye glances.

That sounds really frustrating and awkward, I can imagine how you'd feel. Can you tell me more about what's been going on with Rachel and your boss, like, what exactly has been happening that's making you feel this way?

Okay, so like, yesterday, I was working on this project and Rachel came over and just started questioning my methods, saying I was doing it all wrong, which, honestly, was kinda rude. And then, today, our boss calls me into his office and tells me that Rachel has been complaining about my work, which is just, like, so not true.

Wow, that does sound really unfair and upsetting. I'm guessing you feel like Rachel is trying to sabotage you or something, but before we jump to conclusions, do you think it's possible that there's been some kind of miscommunication or misunderstanding about your work or the project?

Hmm, I don't know, maybe, but it's just so hard to see it that way when it feels like everyone's against me, you know? And it's not just Rachel, there are a few others at work who seem to be, like, ganging up on me or something, which is really making me question whether I even want to keep working there.

I totally get why you'd feel that way, it sounds like a really tough and isolating situation. Have you considered talking to HR about what's been going on, or maybe finding a trusted mentor or colleague who could offer some guidance and support, because it might be helpful to have someone on your side who can help you navigate all this drama?
